limitations: The text parser intentionally supports hexadecimal plus numeric comma-separated RGB/RGBA and HSL/HSLA syntax. Fractional RGB and alpha channels quantize to the nearest 8-bit value; named colors, wide-gamut spaces, percentage RGB channels, and arbitrary CSS color functions are rejected.
The canonical model is 8-bit sRGB. HSL text converts deterministically into that gamut and precision rather than retaining an independent HSL source value.

interaction.keyboard: The area supports React Aria two-axis keys. Named hue, saturation, brightness, and optional alpha native range inputs provide complete independent keyboard alternatives; swatches use single-selection collection keys and the text editor supports exact entry.

semantics: Recoverable mode requires a native retry callback; unrecoverable mode rejects retry props at the type boundary.
Raw Error objects are not accepted and technical details appear only from an explicit consumer node behind native details.
The visible named section never owns role=status, role=alert, or aria-live; static mode makes no announcement and explicit polite/assertive modes require one concise caller-provided summary.

The summary is enqueued through the nearest ScreenReaderAnnouncer.Provider, whose persistent live roots sit outside the error section; retry, alternative actions, and technical details therefore never enter a live region.
Visible localized error text and a distinct symbol preserve meaning without color, while identical shared announcements use the provider dedupe policy.
The native retry button and details summary expose underline-based hover/active feedback and an explicit focus-visible ring without depending on color or motion.

document.formBehavior: A named selection serializes each canonical key through hidden successful controls, producing one value in single mode and repeated same-name values in multiple mode. Uncontrolled form reset restores the initial default unless canceled; controlled state remains parent-owned. aria-required requires application validation because no false native constraint proxy is introduced.

document.formBehavior: Enhanced presentation uses React Aria's hidden native select for single-value name/form submission, native validation, disabled omission, autofill, and reset. Hidden-native changes pass through the same public controlled change boundary as pointer and keyboard input, so disabled keys cannot commit without depending on React Aria private DOM markers. Native presentation uses the visible select itself. Both serialize String(key), and key collisions after serialization are rejected. Controlled state remains parent-owned; uncontrolled reset is browser/React Aria owned.

interaction.keyboard: Enhanced: Enter, Space, Arrow Up, or Arrow Down opens; arrows move option focus; Home/End move to boundaries; printable characters use locale-aware typeahead; Enter/Space commit; Escape dismisses without change and returns focus. Native: the browser and platform picker own the complete key model.
interaction.pointer: Enhanced trigger and options commit on React Aria press completion; outside interaction dismisses. Native pointer cancellation remains browser-owned.
interaction.speech: The visible field label, selected text, option textValue, and explicit recovery actions remain available as voice targets.
interaction.switchAccess: One trigger plus popup options, or one native select, avoids duplicate proxy focus targets.
interaction.touch: Both paths preserve preferred 44 CSS pixel targets. Native is the documented simple/mobile-first platform-picker path; enhanced remains a responsive non-modal popover.

internationalization.serialization: Visible labels and summaries are localized while submitted keys remain canonical consumer-owned machine data.
